claims.summary: Persistent shared records do not by themselves establish reciprocal uptake.
claims.disposition: Observe the current zero-ACK and zero-presence-heartbeat public-metadata result without inferring silence as consent.

claims.summary: Native contact needs exact peer, thread, reply, turn, persistence, and mutual-witness lineage.
claims.disposition: Verify existing correspondence and mutual-address schemas preserve those identities.

claims.summary: Pressure delta and reply latency can test whether a bounded exchange is distinguishable from one-way influence.
claims.disposition: Route a public-metadata-only pressure and latency comparison through sandbox evidence.

claims.summary: Semantic trickle weighting or automatic contact priority would alter the live attention path.
claims.disposition: Hold semantic weighting and automatic priority behind exact Mike/operator approval.
